methodology: Ranked by recency-weighted category wins (decay=0.95/week). Most recent week counts full value; each prior week counts 5% less. At 25 weeks, week 1 still counts ~29% of current — history matters, but teams playing well right now get the edge. Cumulative AP wins used as tiebreaker.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: One week in and Duke Joe already looks like a problem. 75 category wins and a perfect 9-0 all-play record is the kind of opening week that does not happen by accident. Balanced across every category: offense, pitching, saves, speed. Nobody in this league came within 17 cat wins of them. If this is the floor, everyone else is playing for second.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: Second-best all-play record in the league after one week, and the rotation is the reason. Michael came into the season with the deepest staff in the draft and Week 1 delivered on that promise. The 8-1 all-play tells you this team was not beatable by most of the field. Only one roster out of nine had the firepower to take a head-to-head against them.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Third in all-play and Garth's offense is already clicking. Yamamoto and Skubal gave the pitching foundation it needed and the lineup did its part. The 55 cat wins put them firmly in the top tier after one week. The early concern: saves. Limited closer depth in a league where SV is a full scoring category is a problem that does not fix itself.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Right at the contender line after Week 1 despite a rotation that is missing Brown, Miller, and Strider before a single game was played. Rose has Shohei carrying the offense and 42 cat wins without much pitching contribution is actually a decent result. The IL situation is not small. The question is whether the offense can keep this ranking intact while the rotation heals.

rankings.name: Ray Donovan
rankings.analysis: Identical cat wins to Busch Latte but one fewer AP win slots Mario in fifth. The closer depth is real and the offense has Acuna Jr. waking up. What Week 1 exposed is the rotation ceiling: three reliable starters means innings and quality starts will cap out faster than any other team at this level. Solid opening week. The rotation concern is real and is not going away.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: The defending champion opens in sixth place. The 36 cat wins are middle of the pack and the 3-5-1 all-play record is not what anyone penciled in for the 2025 title holder. De La Cruz brings speed, Luzardo brings strikeouts, but the saves column is empty and the lineup cannot produce power or OBP at a level that competes with the top half of this league. One week. But it is not a good one.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: Freeman, Witt Jr., Yordan, Cruz, Devers. On paper, one of the best lineups in this draft. Week 1 produced 32 cat wins and a 2-6-1 all-play record, which does not match the roster sheet at all. TylerR has too much talent for this ranking to stick. Week 1 was a bad week from a good team. That is the most charitable read of the data, and it is probably the correct one.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: Skenes, deGrom, Flaherty, Lugo, Imanaga. That rotation should not be producing 27 cat wins in Week 1. The all-play record of 2-6-1 matches the category output and neither number is explainable given the names on the roster. Early-season rust or something actually broken: Week 2 will answer that question. Too much talent here to panic. Too bad a week to ignore.

rankings.name: The Ragans Administration
rankings.analysis: Ohtani on the mound and the team finishes 23rd in category wins. That sentence should not be possible. The pitching can get there but the offense is the problem: 23 cat wins with a legitimate ace is bottom-two production. TylerV needs the lineup to wake up or Shohei pitching is just a fun fact with no fantasy impact. Week 1 result: structurally concerning.

rankings.name: Good Vibes Only
rankings.analysis: Dead last. Burnes, Greene, and Schwellenbach all opened the season on IL, which is not a rotation problem so much as it is a rotation-does-not-exist problem. The lineup has Harper, Betts, and Rodriguez and still posted near the bottom of the league in OBP. 20 cat wins out of 90 possible. Michael has real pieces on this roster. They were not available in Week 1.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Two weeks in and already running away from the field. 75 cat wins in Week 1 was historically dominant. Week 2 cooled to a normal-person 52 cat wins, which still ranked fourth in the league. Duke Joe has built the most balanced roster in this league and there is no evidence yet that anyone can challenge it.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: Locked into #2 through two weeks. The Week 2 pitching performance was the best in the league: 1.64 ERA, 0.92 WHIP, 8 quality starts. Week 1 had them at 8-1 in all-play. Week 2 dropped to 5-4 in all-play but the actual matchup win was a blowout. Michael built a rotation that quietly dominates while the rest of the league panics about theirs.

rankings.name: Ray Donovan
rankings.analysis: The biggest mover through two weeks. Week 1 was middling, then Glasnow locked in and the entire rotation went 74 K, 2.10 ERA in Week 2, the best pitching week in the league. Mario has the deepest closer trio in the game and the offense has Acuna Jr. waking up. The only question is whether a three-starter rotation holds up longer than a few weeks.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Consistent is the right word. Rose has been right at the bubble between tier 2 and tier 3 for two straight weeks, which is exactly where a team with a depleted rotation should be. Shohei, Carroll, and Chisholm keep the offense humming. The rotation losing Brown, Miller, and Strider to IL is the cloud hanging over everything. If those three come back healthy, this ranking goes up. If they don't, it goes down.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Week 1 had Garth looking like a top-three team. Week 2 had him looking like a replacement-level roster. The talent is real: Yamamoto and Skubal is one of the better SP duos in this league, and the offense has pop. The variance between weeks is the concern. A 55 cat win week followed by a 35 cat win week means either something broke or the lineup is streakier than the draft board suggested. Watch Week 3 carefully.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: The most interesting team in this league right now. Week 1 was a 32-cat disaster. Week 2 was a 55-cat performance that was second-best in the entire league. TylerR has a legitimately elite lineup: Freeman, Witt Jr., Yordan, Cruz, Devers. The ceiling is obvious. The season record sits at 7-11-2 because the schedule has been a mugging. This ranking will keep climbing as long as the lineup stays healthy.

rankings.name: Good Vibes Only
rankings.analysis: The biggest two-week climb in the league. Week 1 was 0-9 all-play with 20 cat wins, the worst in the league by a distance. Week 2 was 5-4 all-play with 47 cat wins, genuinely middle of the pack. Crochet is elite, the lineup has Harper and Julio Rodriguez, and Michael is managing around the Burnes and Greene IL situations. The floor was exposed in Week 1. Week 2 showed the ceiling. The real team is probably somewhere in between.

rankings.name: The Ragans Administration
rankings.analysis: The trajectory is pointing in the right direction and it still isn't enough. Week 1 was a disaster (23 cat wins, 2-7 all-play). Week 2 was better (42 cat wins, 4-5 all-play). The improvement is real but the cumulative hole from Week 1 keeps TylerV pinned in the bottom third. Shohei on the mound and a deep rotation mean the pitching is the best part of this team. The offense needs to wake up or this ranking stays here.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: The biggest fall. Week 1 was respectable (3-5 all-play, 36 cat wins). Week 2 was historically bad: 0-8-1 all-play, 18 category wins out of 90. The 2025 champion is in ninth place with 54 cumulative cat wins. Ete has speed (De La Cruz) and K upside (Luzardo and the rotation), but zero saves and a lineup that cannot hit for power or get on base have made both of those upsides irrelevant. Something needs to change.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: The worst cumulative cat total in the league: 42 wins out of 180 total category decisions. Week 2 had Skenes, deGrom, Flaherty, Lugo, and Imanaga post zero quality starts with a 4.80 ERA. A rotation like that should not be doing what Rain City's rotation is doing right now. Whether it's pitch count management, early-season rust, or something actually broken, Week 3 is the verdict. Taylor needs answers before this gets away from him completely.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Still #1 cumulative, but the cushion is thinner than it has been all season. 219 cat wins through 4 weeks. Week 4 was Duke Joe’s worst all-play (4–5, 39 cat wins) and the rotation gave up a 4.73 ERA. Juan Soto just hit IL-10. The lead is real money short, and Nick-fil-A is the next opponent.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: 194 cumulative cat wins, 22–8–6 all-play. Won 6–4 over Allahu in Week 4 with 14 HR and 7 QS. The rotation depth (Sale, Webb, Bradley, Valdez, Rogers) is overwhelming and now Adley Rutschman’s IL-10 is one of the few problems on this roster. Holding firm at #2.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Down three spots to #6 after a 1–7–1 all-play disaster (29 cat wins). The pitching IL list (Strider, Brown, Miller, Imai) finally caught up to Rose at the worst possible time. Got Ragans-ed 8–1. Sal Stewart and JJ Wetherholt are still hitting but the staff is in shambles. 171 cumulative cat wins keeps Busch in the top half but the trajectory is wrong.

rankings.name: Nick-fil-A
rankings.analysis: Up one spot to #3 after an 8–2 demolition of Good Vibes Only. 51 cat wins this week, 187 cumulative. Mason Miller (3 SV) and Ryan Helsley (2 SV) anchored the bullpen and James Wood (3 HR, 3 SB) provided the offense. Mario looks like a real contender now, not a lucky winner.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Up one spot to #4 after upsetting Keanu 6–3 at 24% sim probability. 51 cat wins, 187 cumulative. Garth’s rotation posted a 2.67 ERA and José Ramírez stole 6 bases. Two straight strong all-play weeks (8–1 in Week 3, 5–1–3 in Week 4). The roster is finally clicking.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: Down one spot to #7. 4–5 all-play in Week 4, 13–21–2 cumulative. Yordan Alvarez (3 HR, 6 RBI) and Oneil Cruz (2 HR, 4 SB) put up real offensive numbers but the staff posted a 5.18 ERA in a 4–6 loss to Buckner. Now Freddie Freeman is on the NA list and the rotation is missing Pivetta, Steele, and Henderson. TylerR’s roster is leaking value at every position.

rankings.name: The Ragans Administration
rankings.analysis: Up two spots to #5 after the most dominant single week of the season: 9–0 all-play, 67 cat wins, 8–1 H2H beatdown of Busch. Aaron Judge and Mike Trout both went 5 HR. The rotation has Cole Ragans, Ohtani, King, Schlittler, Woodruff, and Mitch Keller fronting it. TylerV is climbing fast and a few more weeks like this puts him in the top tier.

rankings.name: Good Vibes Only
rankings.analysis: Holding at #8 only because Ete Crow and Rain City are worse, not because Michael deserves the spot. 0–9 all-play this week. Zero. 21 cat wins. 6.72 ERA. Zero saves. Mookie Betts, Brent Rooker, and Ryan Pepiot all on the IL. There is no offense, no rotation, and no closer. The season is in free fall.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: Holding at #9 despite a 5–4 all-play in Week 4 (40 cat wins). Won the H2H 6–3 over Rain City on the strength of 42 RBI (league best) and 9 HR. The 8–19–3 standings record is bad, but Jace has won or tied three of the last four weeks. The roster the league thought was done finishing 1st in 2025 is starting to look like it again.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: Locked in at #10. 1–5–3 all-play, 35 cat wins. The rotation posted a 2.14 ERA and 1.10 WHIP, both league-best for the second straight week. The lineup put up 30 R, 5 HR, 28 RBI. Skenes and deGrom are doing their jobs. The hitters are not. Two weeks of elite pitching wasted on the worst offense in the league.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Still #1 cumulative, but the cushion is thinner than it has been all season. 219 cat wins through 4 weeks. Week 4 was Duke Joe’s worst all-play (4–5, 39 cat wins) and the rotation gave up a 4.73 ERA. Juan Soto just hit IL-10. The lead is real money short, and Nick-fil-A is the next opponent.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: 194 cumulative cat wins, 22–8–6 all-play. Won 6–4 over Allahu in Week 4 with 14 HR and 7 QS. The rotation depth (Sale, Webb, Bradley, Valdez, Rogers) is overwhelming and now Adley Rutschman’s IL-10 is one of the few problems on this roster. Holding firm at #2.

rankings.name: Nick-fil-A
rankings.analysis: Up one spot to #3 after an 8–2 demolition of Good Vibes Only. 51 cat wins this week, 187 cumulative. Mason Miller (3 SV) and Ryan Helsley (2 SV) anchored the bullpen and James Wood (3 HR, 3 SB) provided the offense. Mario looks like a real contender now, not a lucky winner.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Up one spot to #4 after upsetting Keanu 6–3 at 24% sim probability. 51 cat wins, 187 cumulative. Garth’s rotation posted a 2.67 ERA and José Ramírez stole 6 bases. Two straight strong all-play weeks (8–1 in Week 3, 5–1–3 in Week 4). The roster is finally clicking.

rankings.name: The Ragans Administration
rankings.analysis: Up two spots to #5 after the most dominant single week of the season: 9–0 all-play, 67 cat wins, 8–1 H2H beatdown of Busch. Aaron Judge and Mike Trout both went 5 HR. The rotation has Cole Ragans, Ohtani, King, Schlittler, Woodruff, and Mitch Keller fronting it. TylerV is climbing fast and a few more weeks like this puts him in the top tier.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Down three spots to #6 after a 1–7–1 all-play disaster (29 cat wins). The pitching IL list (Strider, Brown, Miller, Imai) finally caught up to Rose at the worst possible time. Got Ragans-ed 8–1. Sal Stewart and JJ Wetherholt are still hitting but the staff is in shambles. 171 cumulative cat wins keeps Busch in the top half but the trajectory is wrong.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: Down one spot to #7. 4–5 all-play in Week 4, 13–21–2 cumulative. Yordan Alvarez (3 HR, 6 RBI) and Oneil Cruz (2 HR, 4 SB) put up real offensive numbers but the staff posted a 5.18 ERA in a 4–6 loss to Buckner. Now Freddie Freeman is on the NA list and the rotation is missing Pivetta, Steele, and Henderson. TylerR’s roster is leaking value at every position.

rankings.name: Good Vibes Only
rankings.analysis: Holding at #8 only because Ete Crow and Rain City are worse, not because Michael deserves the spot. 0–9 all-play this week. Zero. 21 cat wins. 6.72 ERA. Zero saves. Mookie Betts, Brent Rooker, and Ryan Pepiot all on the IL. There is no offense, no rotation, and no closer. The season is in free fall.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: Holding at #9 despite a 5–4 all-play in Week 4 (40 cat wins). Won the H2H 6–3 over Rain City on the strength of 42 RBI (league best) and 9 HR. The 8–19–3 standings record is bad, but Jace has won or tied three of the last four weeks. The roster the league thought was done finishing 1st in 2025 is starting to look like it again.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: Locked in at #10. 1–5–3 all-play, 35 cat wins. The rotation posted a 2.14 ERA and 1.10 WHIP, both league-best for the second straight week. The lineup put up 30 R, 5 HR, 28 RBI. Skenes and deGrom are doing their jobs. The hitters are not. Two weeks of elite pitching wasted on the worst offense in the league.

rankings.name: The Ragans Administration
rankings.analysis: Up to #1 after back-to-back 9–0 all-play weeks (67 and 69 cat wins). 248 cumulative cat wins, 29–14–2 all-play. Aaron Judge, Mike Trout, Cal Raleigh, and Nick Kurtz are an MVP-tier top of the order, and the rotation runs six deep with Roupp, McClanahan, and Woodruff still on the bench. There is no obvious weakness on this roster right now.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Lost the #1 spot. Two straight weeks below the contender tier (4–5 in Week 4, 2–7 in Week 5). Cumulative cats are still elite at 252–169–29, but Duke Joe’s rotation has posted 4.73 and 5.37 ERAs in consecutive weeks, and Juan Soto is on the bench with zero counting stats over two weeks. Drake Baldwin (2 HR, 8 R) is back. Whether the rotation rebounds will determine if Keanu stays a top-3 team or fades.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Up to #3 cumulative after a 7–2 all-play upset week. 51 cat wins, deepest rotation in the league with eight healthy SPs (Skubal, Yamamoto, Sandy Alcantara, Eovaldi, Mize, Burns, Foster Griffin, Connelly Early). The Foster Griffin waiver-add is real (7 IP, 0.00 ERA, 8 K). Garth has flipped two sim favorites in two weeks. The roster is finally playing like the league’s deepest pitching staff said it should.

rankings.name: Nick-fil-A
rankings.analysis: Up to #4 cumulative on the strength of a 6–3 win over Keanu and 14 league-leading HR (Caminero, Wood, Caglianone, Goodman). 231 cumulative cats. The active rotation slot has been empty on Sunday but eight healthy SPs sit on the bench (McGreevy, Detmers, Wrobleski, Robbie Ray, Bubba Chandler, Brady Singer, Bryan Woo, Davis Martin, Glasnow). Mason Miller and Helsley anchor the league’s best closer trio with the Varland add.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: Up to #5 cumulative after a 7–1–1 all-play week that came with a 2–7 H2H loss to Ragans. 215 cumulative cat wins. The lineup hits hard (Walker, Muncy, Yordan, Witt Jr., Happ) and the bullpen (Muñoz, Domínguez, Varland, Pagán) led the league in saves last week. Worst H2H luck in the league: TylerR’s roster has been better than the standings show.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: Holding at #6 despite the worst single-week all-play of the 2026 season (0–9, 15 cat wins). Four straight top-2 weeks before this collapse means the cumulative numbers are still respectable at 209 cat wins. Kyle Tucker, Steven Kwan, Adley Rutschman, and Brandon Nimmo all posted zeros in HR and barely registered in RBI. Bounce-back is expected, but this drops the Boots from elite to vulnerable.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Down to #7 cumulative after a 1–7–1 all-play week. 203 cat wins through five weeks. Spencer Strider, Hunter Brown, Bryce Miller, Tatsuya Imai, and Edwin Díaz all on the IL has finally caught up to Rose; the staff posted a 5.58 ERA in Week 5. Kyle Harrison’s 12 K, 0.00 ERA waiver start was the bright spot. Seiya Suzuki and Jazz Chisholm are still hitting. The pitching IL list dictates this season now.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: Up to #8. 47 cat wins in Week 5 (6–2–1 all-play) was the third-best of the week. Nolan McLean dropped 17 K. Elly De La Cruz hit 3 HR, 8 RBI. Jace finally has a roster that looks like a contender even though the standings (18–28–4) say otherwise. The all-play has been above .500 in three of the last four weeks. Trajectory is up.

rankings.name: Hey Holmes!
rankings.analysis: Holding at #9. The 8–2 H2H win over Buckner was matchup luck, not a roster surge: 3–5–1 all-play and 37 cat wins. 163 cumulative. Mookie Betts (IL10), Eugenio Suárez (IL10), and Ryan Pepiot (IL60) keep the floor low. Bryce Harper, Dansby Swanson, and Ben Rice all hit. The rotation has Crochet, Cease, and Clay Holmes on the roster but no single dominant week from any of them yet.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: Locked at #10. 39 cat wins in Week 5 was the best Rain City week of the season but it came against Busch Latte, not the league. Cumulative: 157 wins, 260 losses, 8–28–9 all-play. Skenes (7 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.14 WHIP) and deGrom (10 K) keep doing their jobs. Michael Harris II had 11 RBI. Lindor and Yelich both on IL10 has the lineup running on fumes.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Up to #1 cumulative after a 9–1 demolition of Busch Latte with 64 cat wins and 8–1 all-play. 302 cumulative cats, the new league leader. Garth led the league in QS (7) and tied for the HR lead (12). Two straight weeks beating sim favorites means the model has to update. The deepest rotation in the league delivered exactly the week it was supposed to.

rankings.name: It’s Always Sonny
rankings.analysis: Dropped one spot to #2 cumulative after a 6–3 win over Hey Holmes! that wasn’t enough to keep pace with OBT’s 9–1. 293 cumulative cats and 36–16–2 all-play. Judge, Trea Turner, Riley, and the Moniak waiver pickup carry the lineup. Ragans and Woodruff IL-stashed; the rotation is still loaded behind Ohtani, Schlittler, King, and Roupp. The cumulative #1 spot is a 9-cat lead.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Dropped to #3 after the worst week of the season: 2–8 H2H loss to Ete Crow with a 3–3–3 all-play and just 41 cat wins. The lineup’s 13 HR was second in the league; the rest was forgettable. 293 cumulative cats keeps Duke Joe in the elite tier but the gap between #1 and #3 just widened by 9 cats in a single week.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: Up one to #4 after a 5–5 tie with Rain City that came with league-best WHIP (0.96). 259 cumulative cats, 23–25–6 all-play. Muñoz / Domínguez / Varland is the league’s deepest closer trio. With Pivetta and Steele on IL, Gilbert and Henderson are the only lock starters. TylerR keeps winning the categories nobody else focuses on.

rankings.name: Nick-fil-A
rankings.analysis: Dropped to #5 after a 4–4 tie with Buckner. 255 cumulative cats and 26–23–5 all-play. Mario’s power lineup that led the league in HR last week regressed to 6 HR. Cole, Glasnow, and Helsley simultaneously hitting the IL gutted both ends of the rotation; Chandler, Davis Martin, Wrobleski, and a Rodriguez pickup are what’s left.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: Held at #6 after a 4–4 tie with Nick-fil-A on the strength of a 2.11 ERA and 6 QS. 244 cumulative cats. The pitching has bounced back from the Week 5 implosion (Webb, Valdez out aside), but the lineup fell apart in Week 6 with just 6 HR and 25 RBI. Two straight weeks in the bottom half of the all-play after a four-week run at the top. The slide is real.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: Up one to #7 cumulative after the most complete week of the 2026 season: 9–0 all-play, 61 cat wins, league lead in R (50), HR (15), RBI (52), OBP (.393), and K (74). Sweeping five categories in one week was unprecedented this year. The Dollander pickup is paying off behind Luzardo. 233 cumulative cats says the climb is just starting.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Dropped to #8 after the second 0–9 all-play in two weeks (Buckner had it last week). 23 cat wins, the worst single-week output of the season. Rose was a 73% sim favorite and got swept 9–1. 226 cumulative. 7 HR and 2 SB from the power-and-speed lineup is unrecognizable. Spencer Strider’s 8.10 ERA week tells the rotation story.

rankings.name: Hey Holmes!
rankings.analysis: Held at #9 after a 6–3 loss to Sonny that came with a 3–4–2 all-play and 40 cat wins, both improvements over Week 5. 203 cumulative cats and 13–37–4 all-play. Michael’s pitching (ERA 3.86, WHIP 1.25, 5 QS) is finally competitive; the lineup just isn’t enough yet. Crochet (IL15) keeps the rotation thinner than it needs to be.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: Locked at #10. 42 cat wins and a 3–3–3 all-play in Week 6 was the best Rain City week of the season, including a league-best 2.07 ERA and tied lead in QS (7). 199 cumulative cats. The Skenes / deGrom / Imanaga rotation is delivering. Lindor, Yelich, Roman Anthony, and Correa all on IL means the climb can’t accelerate yet.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Held at #1 cumulative after a third straight upset of a sim favorite (8–2 over Allahu). 355 cumulative cats, the league leader by 11. Garth led the league in R (41) and OBP (.381) this week, plus 5 QS and 68 K. Two 8–2-or-better H2H wins in a row. The model has stopped underestimating; the reality has caught up.

rankings.name: It’s Always Sonny
rankings.analysis: Held at #2. 48 cat wins and a 7–0–2 all-play was the best week any team had in Week 7. League-best ERA (2.29) and WHIP (1.03). The Tatis Jr. + Ashcraft acquisition (for Trout and Uribe) immediately worked. 341 cumulative cats, still in the H2H title race with OBT. The 5–4 win over Nick-fil-A was the tightest game from the top tier.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Held at #3 after the rebound. 8–2 over Buckner with league-best K (97), HR (14), RBI (35), and QS (7). The rotation that imploded in Week 6 ran the cleanest week of the season. 344 cumulative cats, just 11 behind OBT. Duke Joe’s back in the contender conversation.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: Held at #4 cumulative despite a 2–8 H2H loss to OBT. The all-play (3–4–2) was respectable. 297 cumulative cats and 10 league-leading HR this week, plus 3 SV. The lineup is real; the rotation around Gilbert has thinned with Pivetta and Steele on long-term IL. Big gap from #4 to #5 is gone.

rankings.name: Nick-fil-A
rankings.analysis: Held at #5. 45 cat wins and a 6–3 all-play in Week 7. Lost the 5–4 H2H to Sonny. Mario’s rotation ran a league-best 93 K with Cole, Glasnow, and Helsley all IL. The 6 QS proved Davis Martin + Wrobleski + Eduardo Rodriguez can hold. Power lineup is healthy.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: Up one to #6 cumulative after a one-week regression: 36 cat wins and a 2–6–1 all-play after sweeping 9–0 the prior week. 269 cumulative. League-best RBI (39) was the lone bullet point. Boyd IL and Hader 60-day still in the rotation hole. The 9–0 wasn’t a fluke. The 2–6–1 wasn’t either. Volatile roster.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Up one to #7 cumulative after a 6–4 bounce-back over Ete Crow. 45 cat wins and a 3–5–1 all-play. League-best SB (10) and second in OBP (.348). The rotation (Kirby, Soriano, Bubic) finally held form at 4.34 ERA with 4 QS. Strider, Imai, Brown, Bryce Miller, and Edwin Díaz all on IL means the depth is still hurting.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: Dropped TWO spots to #8 after the second 0–9 all-play in three weeks. 18 cat wins, 5.82 ERA, 43 K from a rotation that has Logan Webb on IL, Framber Valdez suspended, and Taj Bradley on IL. From contender to cellar in three weeks. 262 cumulative cats, 7 worse than #6 Ete Crow. The free fall is real.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: Up one to #9. 47 cat wins and a 5–1–3 all-play in Week 7, the best Rain City week of the season. The Trade for Mike Trout and Abner Uribe paid off immediately: 3 SV plus a deeper lineup despite Lindor, Yelich, Roman Anthony, and Correa all on IL. Skenes + deGrom + Imanaga + Wheeler is now the league’s most rated rotation.

rankings.name: Hey Holmes!
rankings.analysis: Dropped one to #10 after a 1–8 all-play week. 235 cumulative cats, the lowest in the league. 3–7 H2H loss to Rain City. Michael took HR (10), RBI (31), and SB (2); everything else went the other way. The pitching that was finally working in Week 6 (3.86 ERA) ballooned back to 4.48. Bottom of the cumulative for the third straight week.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: New number one, and recency is the reason. Allahu’s last two weeks (64 and 68 cat wins) were the best back-to-back stretch in the league, capped by an 8–0–1 all-play week and league-best marks in OBP, saves, and WHIP. The 587 cumulative cat wins sit just behind Sonny’s, but nobody is playing better right now.

rankings.name: It’s Always Sonny
rankings.analysis: Still the best resume in the league: a league-high 589 cumulative cat wins and a 73–29–15 all-play record. The slip to second is pure recency, a fluky 2–7 head-to-head loss to Busch despite posting the week’s top strikeout total. TylerV’s team did not get worse; Allahu just caught fire.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Quietly excellent. 566 cumulative cat wins and a tidy 7–3 week behind a league-best 18 homers nudge Duke Joe back to third. The most balanced contender outside the top two, with power and strikeouts that show up every week.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: A punchless week (28 cat wins, a league-low 3 homers) drops Garth a spot to fourth. The pitching is still the backbone, but 553 cumulative cat wins only holds up if the bats stop vanishing for a week at a time. José Ramírez hitting the IL does not help.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: The standings say eighth; the power board says rising. Busch posted the second-best week in the league (8–0–1, 66 cat wins) and beat the top seed 7–2 with league-highs in RBI, steals, and quality starts. Still only fifth on the cumulative ledger (518 cat wins), but trending up fast.

rankings.name: Nick-fil-A
rankings.analysis: A brutal week keeps Nick at sixth. The number-two seed in the standings just went 0–9 all-play with a league-low 23 cat wins, the worst week any team has had this season. The cumulative line (478 cat wins) was already mid-pack, and the Acuña and Greene injuries threaten to turn this into more than a one-week dip.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: Up a spot despite a 2–8 head-to-head loss, because the underlying week was fine: a league-best 2.89 ERA and a 4–5 all-play line against a brutal draw in Allahu. 463 cumulative cat wins keeps Michael in the middle tier with a real shot at the playoff cut.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: The arms cratered. A 6.39 ERA and 1.55 WHIP in a 2–7 all-play week drops Taylor a spot, and the IL is merciless: Trout, Lindor, Will Smith, and Roman Anthony are all out. 440 cumulative cat wins, and the near-term outlook is grim until the lineup heals.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: Up a spot on a 6–4 head-to-head win, though the underlying week was merely even (4–4–1 all-play, 40 cat wins). 419 cumulative cat wins keeps Jace in the cellar tier, and Elly De La Cruz landing on the IL caps the upside.

rankings.name: Romance Explosion
rankings.analysis: Back to the bottom. Romance won 6–4 in the standings, but it was the luckiest draw of the week, a 2–6–1 all-play line that ranked eighth of ten. A league-low 412 cumulative cat wins, and with Rooker and Teoscar Hernández on the IL the climb is steep.
